chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI SCRAs 186 and 187 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of main case 2. For hearing of CMA No.2231/2025 13.11.2025 Mr. Muhammad Khalil Dogar, advocate for the applicant On 23.10.2025, following order was passed: “The impugned judgment appears to be rendered on the basis of record / evidence. The questions proposed for determination appears to be argumentative and / or agitating factual controversy, which cannot be articulated to be amenable to the reference jurisdiction. Learned counsel seeks time. At his request to come up on 30.10.2025. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in the connected file.” On 30.10.2025, following order was passed: On the last date, i.e. 23.10.2025, following order was passed:- “The impugned judgment appears to be rendered on the basis of record / evidence. The questions proposed for determination appears to be argumentative and / or agitating factual controversy, which cannot be articulated to be amenable to the reference jurisdiction. Learned counsel seeks time. At his request to come up on 30.10.2025. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in the connected file.” Today, learned counsel for the applicant once again fails to assist. Let Collector, Collectorate of Customs Enforcement be present in person on the next date to assist. To come up on 13.11.2025. Office is instructed to place a copy of this order in the connected SCRA listed above. Today Mr. Amjad Hussain Rajpar, Additional Collector, Hyderabad, is present and states that these reference applications may be dismissed as withdrawn.

Today Mr. Amjad Hussain Rajpar, Additional Collector, Hyderabad, is present and states that these reference applications may be dismissed as withdrawn. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in connected matter. Judge Judge Amjad
